close_reason: Positive observation: Zeroizing token with documented response leak is correct
description:
lib.rs:103-105 stores the auth token as Zeroizing<String>. Module docs (lib.rs:46-58) explicitly document that the reqwest response Bytes buffer is NOT zeroed and that this is unavoidable at the reqwest layer.

A future contributor or reviewer might suggest 'just wrap the response body in Zeroizing too' — this is wrong. reqwest hands back bytes::Bytes, which is reference-counted and cannot be zeroized in-place; wrapping at the receiving end zeros only the Vec, not the underlying buffer reqwest holds. The doc comment is accurate AND honest about a real limitation. Defending against 'just zeroize everything' is correct.

The only way to fix the response-buffer leak is to drop reqwest in favor of a streaming HTTP client that hands out a Zeroizing buffer — too much complexity for this crate's scope.

Defend against: 'why not also zeroize the response body / put body / parsed serde_json::Value' — those are mostly impossible (Bytes ref-counted) or actively harmful (intermediate trees would double the surface, not reduce it).
title: DECISION: Zeroizing token + documented response-buffer leak is honest and correct
